articles.title: Weekly poll results: Android 17 with better security and AirDrop support has people excited
articles.description: Last week, we asked you about which announcements coming out of Google I/O had you buzzing with excitement – in general, but also specifically for the upcoming Android 17 update. Starting with the top-level question, Android 17 was by far the best thing demoed at I/O 2026. Not surprising, given our audience. But the ranking of the other options did surprise us – barely anyone cared about Wear OS 7. The smartwatches lost to Gemini Intelligence and Android Auto (which were essentially tied) and even the new Googlebook design (the Android-powered laptops) sparked more interest. Some...

articles.title: ♊ Gemini, Weekly Horoscope (June 1 - June 7, 2026): Communication and opportunities can help in positive movement | Love, Career, and Finance Predictions You Cannot Afford to Miss
articles.description: The Gemini weekly horoscope indicates an encouraging week for Gemini natives. The Moon will transit into the Sagittarius sign on 1st June 2026 (evening). This can bring focus to your relationships and partnerships. The Moon will transit into the Capricorn sign on 4th June 2026. This can increase your practical thinking. On 6th June 2026, the Moon will transit into the Aquarius sign. This transit can improve your future planning. Mercury and Venus are in the Gemini sign. This can strengthen your communication and social interactions. Discover what Anand Sagar Pathak of Astropatri reveals in this weekly horoscope for Gemini, offering valuable insights aligned with the Gemini horoscope this week and the broader weekly astrology predictions for Gemini.

articles.title: Daily Libra Horoscope: Courage rises but caution pays; avoid big buys and sharp arguments
articles.description: Today, a wave of boldness may inspire you to make fresh moves, but tread carefully to dodge impulsive decisions. Anticipate possible hiccups in travel and a shake-up in your schedule. Fiscal responsibility should be your mantra today, and it’s advisable to delay any major car purchases.

articles.title: Weekly deals: the Galaxy A57 gets its first real discount, Galaxy A37 and S25 FE also on sale
articles.description: It took a while, but the Samsung Galaxy A57 is finally up on Amazon – and it kicks things off with a $60 discount. That’s a bit less than the A37 discount, but it’s enough to shift the balance of power between the two. The A37 is the cheaper model and if you can live with 6GB of RAM and 128GB non-expandable storage, then yes, it is meaningfully cheaper. But the 256GB models of the A37 and A57 are currently only $10 apart. Samsung Galaxy A57 $60 off Read our review ...

articles.title: Nvidia CFO Colette Kress at company's earnings call: AI is no longer nice-to-have as ...
articles.description: Nvidia's CFO, Colette Kress, declared AI a business necessity, not a luxury, driving demand across the AI stack. The chip giant is restructuring its reporting into Data Center and Edge Computing segments to reflect this growth.

articles.title: Indian cuts export duties on petrol, diesel and aviation turbine fuel
articles.description: India will cut its export duty on petrol diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) for the fortnight starting June 1, its government said in a statement on Saturday.

articles.title: Kick’s new feature lets streamers hide live viewer numbers from streams
articles.description: Kick now lets streamers hide live viewer counts, a move aiming to ease pressure on creators, especially smaller ones. This optional feature shifts focus from raw popularity, potentially influencing how streams are recommended. While supporters hail it as a boost for new talent, critics worry about losing a gauge of trending content.

articles.description: WordPress.com and Weebly are both dependable and affordable ways to quickly build a website, but which one is better? I compare the two website builders to help you select a foundation for your personal blog, online business, and everything in between.

articles.title: Mystery Beats headphones appear around Lamine Yamal's neck
articles.description: A new pair of Beats headphones are on the way, as a new unnamed model surfaces in a series of Instagram posts by football star Lamine Yamal. A still from a video showing the new unannounced Beats headphones - Image Credit: Lamine Yamal On May 23, some mystery headphones appeared in an FCC filing , indicating a new pair was coming out of Cupertino. One week later, an Instagram post has shown that it's from Beats. The shots on the official account for Lamine Yamal showed the sporting celebrity wearing and carrying around some bright pink headphones. In the four photographs and one video, the headphones are either around his neck or hanging off a bag to his side. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums

articles.title: Indie App Spotlight: ‘NextThere’ helps you navigate public transit with rich insights
articles.description: Welcome to Indie App Spotlight . This is a weekly 9to5Mac series where we showcase the latest apps in the indie app world. If you’re a developer and would like your app featured, get in contact . Navigating across different cities and figuring out their public transportation can sometimes be a bit messy. Maps apps might not always have the best information, or the apps that do exist aren’t presenting data in a super readable manner. NextThere aims to tackle that problem, and also packs loads of historical data for providing more informed information regarding delays, on-time performance, and so on. It’s great for tourists and frequent commuters alike. more...

articles.title: More than 120 deeptech startups head to France for Bharat Innovates
articles.description: Over 120 Indian deeptech startups will showcase their innovations at the Bharat Innovates event in Nice, France, from June 14-16. This initiative, part of the India-France year of Innovation, aims to foster global partnerships and attract investment for India's emerging tech ecosystem. The event will highlight advancements across 13 frontier technology themes.

articles.title: CUET-UG exam hit by delay after TCS technical glitch
articles.description: Tata Consultancy Services reported a two-hour delay in the morning shift of the CUET-UG examination due to a brief technical issue. The problem was resolved, and the exam resumed with compensatory time, ensuring the integrity of the process. TCS is working with the NTA to ensure smooth conduct of future computer-based tests.

articles.title: Operation Sindoor 2.0: How the army is preparing for the next battle
articles.description: India's Army is undergoing a significant transformation post-Operation Sindoor, shifting towards preemptive, technology-driven deterrence. New formations like Rudra Brigades and Bhairav battalions, alongside integrated drone units, are enhancing combat readiness. This modernization focuses on unmanned systems, AI, and rapid response, reflecting a decisive move from manpower-heavy tactics to a high-tech, multi-domain force.

articles.title: Vadodara's GSV: A unique transport university boosting Gujarat's growth
articles.description: Gati Shakti Vishwavidyalaya in Vadodara is India's first central university for transport and logistics. It fosters innovation through industry collaborations, offers diverse courses, and boasts high placement rates, contributing to India's development.
